req.title: Joint account operations (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-093 covers three distinct testable behaviours: (1) a primary joint-account holder successfully performs a transaction (positive/EP), (2) a secondary holder is denied ownership modification with HTTP 403 (negative/NEG+DT), (3) idempotent re-submission of the same Idempotency-Key returns the original result without double-applying the operation (boundary/BVA on the idempotency key boundary between first and duplicate submission). A fourth test covers the state transition from 'pending first submission' to 'completed' to 'idempotent repeat' (STC). Together these give full coverage of the requirement's happy path, error pathway, and idempotency invariant.

tests.name: test_primary_holder_transaction_succeeds
preconditions: A joint account exists with id JOINT_ACCOUNT_ID
PRIMARY_TOKEN is a valid auth token for the primary holder
The account has sufficient balance for the transaction
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with primary holder credentials and a valid transaction payload
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body reflects the applied transaction amount
trace: R-FIN-093: 'A joint-account holder shall perform transactions'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_primary_holder_transaction_succeeds(api_client, joint_account, primary_auth_headers):
    account_id = joint_account["id"]
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"type": "debit", "amount": 100, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {**primary_auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transaction_id" in body, "Missing transaction_id in response"
    assert body.get("amount") == 100

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_ownership_change_forbidden
preconditions: A joint account exists with id JOINT_ACCOUNT_ID
SECONDARY_TOKEN is a valid auth token for the secondary holder
The secondary holder role is restricted from modifying ownership
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with secondary holder credentials and an ownership-change payload
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating ownership modification is not permitted
trace: R-FIN-093: 'the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership'
R-FIN-093: Error pathway '403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_secondary_holder_ownership_change_forbidden(api_client, joint_account, secondary_auth_headers):
    account_id = joint_account["id"]
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    ownership_payload = {"action": "transfer_ownership", "new_owner_id": "user-999"}
    headers = {**secondary_auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        json=ownership_payload,
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403 but got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body, "Response must include error detail"
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ("forbidden", "ownership", "not permitted"))

tests.name: test_idempotent_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result
preconditions: A joint account exists with id JOINT_ACCOUNT_ID
PRIMARY_TOKEN is a valid auth token for the primary holder
The same Idempotency-Key has NOT been used before (fresh per test run)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with primary holder credentials, valid transaction payload, and a unique Idempotency-Key (first submission)
Record the HTTP status and full response body
POST the identical request again with the same Idempotency-Key (duplicate submission)
Record the second HTTP status and full response body
expected: Second submission returns the same HTTP status as the first
Second response body is identical to first response body (same transaction_id, amount, timestamps)
The account balance is debited only once (no double-apply)
trace: R-FIN-093: 'duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result'
BVA: boundary is exactly the second submission using an identical key (n=1 duplicate)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_idempotent_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result(api_client, joint_account, primary_auth_headers):
    account_id = joint_account["id"]
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"type": "debit", "amount": 50, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {**primary_auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    first = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code in (200, 201), f"First call failed: {first.status_code}"
    first_body = first.json()

    second = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == first.status_code, "Idempotent call must return same status"
    second_body = second.json()

    assert second_body["transaction_id"] == first_body["transaction_id"], "transaction_id must be identical"
    assert second_body["amount"] == first_body["amount"], "amount must be identical"

    balance = get_account_balance(api_client, account_id, primary_auth_headers)
    expected_balance = joint_account["initial_balance"] - 50
    assert balance == expected_balance, f"Balance debited twice: got {balance}, expected {expected_balance}"

tests.name: test_state_transition_pending_to_completed_to_idempotent_repeat
preconditions: A joint account exists in ACTIVE state
PRIMARY_TOKEN is a valid auth token for the primary holder
No prior transaction with the chosen Idempotency-Key exists
actions: Submit transaction (state: PENDING -> COMPLETED on first call)
Verify state is COMPLETED in response
Re-submit same request with same Idempotency-Key (state should remain COMPLETED, not re-enter PENDING)
Verify idempotent response still reports COMPLETED with original transaction data
expected: First call: status field is 'completed' (or equivalent terminal state)
Second call: status field is still 'completed' — not 'pending' or a new state
No new transaction record created on second call
trace: R-FIN-093: idempotency invariant preserves terminal state across duplicate submissions
STC: covers PENDING -> COMPLETED -> (idempotent repeat stays COMPLETED) transition arc
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_state_transition_pending_to_completed_to_idempotent_repeat(api_client, joint_account, primary_auth_headers):
    account_id = joint_account["id"]
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"type": "credit", "amount": 200, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {**primary_auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    first = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code in (200, 201)
    first_body = first.json()
    assert first_body.get("status", "").lower() in ("completed", "success", "applied")
    original_txn_id = first_body["transaction_id"]

    second = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == first.status_code
    second_body = second.json()
    assert second_body.get("status", "").lower() in ("completed", "success", "applied")
    assert second_body["transaction_id"] == original_txn_id, "Duplicate must return original txn_id, not a new one"

tests.name: test_decision_table_role_action_matrix
preconditions: Both primary and secondary holder tokens are available
Decision table covers: role x action combinations
actions: Primary holder performs allowed transaction -> expect 200/201
Secondary holder performs allowed transaction -> expect 200/201
Primary holder attempts ownership change -> expect 200/201 (primary is permitted)
Secondary holder attempts ownership change -> expect 403
trace: R-FIN-093: 'A joint-account holder shall perform transactions' (both holders)
R-FIN-093: 'secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership'
DT: exhausts role x action decision combinations
